office_does: A state representative writes and votes on state laws and budgets, conducts oversight, and represents their legislative district.
race_blurb: Second-term representative Joe Timmons emphasizes local investment and public services; military and law-enforcement veteran Justin Pike emphasizes behavioral-health-based safety and government accountability.

candidates.summary: Timmons is a second-term representative emphasizing public investment, education, housing, health-care access, and living-wage jobs.
highlights: Supports education, housing, and health-care access [S1]
Reports securing state funding for flood protection and community facilities [S1]

spending.basis: His platform and record emphasize public investment and securing state funding for local projects.

experience.basis: He is a second-term representative with prior government-relations and executive-branch experience.

candidates.summary: Pike is a first-time candidate and military and law-enforcement veteran emphasizing behavioral-health-based safety and government accountability.
highlights: Supports addressing behavioral health as part of public safety [S1]
Supports greater government accountability and transparency [S1]

spending.basis: He emphasizes state-government accountability and transparency rather than program expansion.

safety.basis: His law-enforcement formation and behavioral-health emphasis combine enforcement and service approaches.

experience.basis: He has substantial military, law-enforcement, and guild leadership experience but no public-office record.
